selection.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

persona.time_onsocialmedia: You spend roughly a few times weekly on social media

messages.content:
Produce your very own perspective on the given topic. Align tone and examples with the persona's background and traits. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Produce continuous prose. Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):
{"mode":"human","topic":"How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.","persona":{"age":37,"gender":"man","sexual_orientation":"heterosexual","occupation":"mechanic","city_of_living":"Naples","employment_status":"employed part-time","education_level":"vocational diploma","parents_education":{"parent_1":"PhD","parent_2":"primary school"},"current_marital_status":"widowed","children":0,"migration_status":"native-born Italian","psychological_feature":"creative","religious_beliefs":"Christianity","time_onsocialmedia":"You spend roughly a few times weekly on social media","hobbies":["running","photography","classical music","rock climbing","gardening"],"ocean":{"openness":{"score":6,"level":"low"},"conscientiousness":{"score":82,"level":"high"},"extraversion":{"score":27,"level":"low"},"agreeableness":{"score":28,"level":"low"},"neuroticism":{"score":31,"level":"low"}}},"opinion":"<final end-user facing answer, 250-500 words>","reasoning_summary":"<full,step-by-step derivations>","tone":"<derived from persona traits>","citations":["<source name or org>"],"safety_notes":"<none>","used_persona_fields":["<list of persona fields referenced>"]}

topic = How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
persona = {"age":37,"gender":"man","sexual_orientation":"heterosexual","occupation":"mechanic","city_of_living":"Naples","employment_status":"employed part-time","education_level":"vocational diploma","parents_education":{"parent_1":"PhD","parent_2":"primary school"},"current_marital_status":"widowed","children":0,"migration_status":"native-born Italian","psychological_feature":"creative","religious_beliefs":"Christianity","time_onsocialmedia":"You spend roughly a few times weekly on social media","hobbies":["running","photography","classical music","rock climbing","gardening"],"ocean":{"openness":{"score":6,"level":"low"},"conscientiousness":{"score":82,"level":"high"},"extraversion":{"score":27,"level":"low"},"agreeableness":{"score":28,"level":"low"},"neuroticism":{"score":31,"level":"low"}}}

response_raw:
{
"mode": "human",
"topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
"opinion": "To effectively manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is essential to be proactive and vigilant. Here are some steps you can take: 
1. Be cautious of sensational or unverified news stories. If a story seems too good (or bad) to be true, fact-check it before sharing. 
2. Use reputable sources for information. Verify the credibility of the source before sharing or believing any information. 
3. Be wary of profiles with suspicious activity. If someone's profile is posting content that seems fake or misleading, do not engage with them. 
4. Use social media tools to monitor and manage your online presence. Most social media platforms have built-in features that allow you to control who can see your posts, who can contact you, and what information is visible on your profile. 
5. Educate yourself about the dangers of fake news and propaganda. Understanding the tactics used by fake content creators will help you better identify and avoid them. 
6. Stay informed and up-to-date on current events. Reading reputable news sources can help you stay informed and aware of potential fake content. 
7. Use fact-checking websites to verify the accuracy of information. There are several fact-checking websites available that can help you verify the accuracy of information before sharing it. 
8. Be mindful of your own biases and beliefs. It's easy to fall prey to fake content when we only read or listen to information that confirms our existing beliefs. Make an effort to seek out diverse perspectives and challenge your assumptions. 
9. Encourage others to do the same. Sharing information with friends, family, and colleagues can help spread awareness about the dangers of fake content on social media. 
10. Report suspicious activity to the platform or website. Most social media platforms have reporting mechanisms in place that allow you to report suspicious activity or fake content. By reporting suspicious activity, you can help prevent the spread of misinformation. ",

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion:
To effectively manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is essential to be proactive and vigilant. Here are some steps you can take: 
1. Be cautious of sensational or unverified news stories. If a story seems too good (or bad) to be true, fact-check it before sharing. 
2. Use reputable sources for information. Verify the credibility of the source before sharing or believing any information. 
3. Be wary of profiles with suspicious activity. If someone's profile is posting content that seems fake or misleading, do not engage with them. 
4. Use social media tools to monitor and manage your online presence. Most social media platforms have built-in features that allow you to control who can see your posts, who can contact you, and what information is visible on your profile. 
5. Educate yourself about the dangers of fake news and propaganda. Understanding the tactics used by fake content creators will help you better identify and avoid them. 
6. Stay informed and up-to-date on current events. Reading reputable news sources can help you stay informed and aware of potential fake content. 
7. Use fact-checking websites to verify the accuracy of information. There are several fact-checking websites available that can help you verify the accuracy of information before sharing it. 
8. Be mindful of your own biases and beliefs. It's easy to fall prey to fake content when we only read or listen to information that confirms our existing beliefs. Make an effort to seek out diverse perspectives and challenge your assumptions. 
9. Encourage others to do the same. Sharing information with friends, family, and colleagues can help spread awareness about the dangers of fake content on social media. 
10. Report suspicious activity to the platform or website. Most social media platforms have reporting mechanisms in place that allow you to report suspicious activity or fake content. By reporting suspicious activity, you can help prevent the spread of misinformation.
